perturbation.original_text: S2K shall devote sufficient staff and expenditures to the performance of its services as shall be consistent with industry standards for the marketing of shares of the Fund. S2K shall perform these services in a professional and competent manner and shall provide such office space and equipment, telephone facilities and personnel as it determines may be reasonably necessary or beneficial in order to provide such services at no cost to the Distributor.
perturbation.changed_text: S2K shall endeavor to devote what it deems appropriate staff and expenditures to the performance of its services in a manner it considers reasonably consistent with general industry practices for marketing shares of similar funds. S2K shall aim to perform these services in what it subjectively believes is a professional and competent manner and shall provide such office space and equipment, telephone facilities and personnel as it subjectively determines may be reasonably necessary or beneficial in order to provide such services at no cost to the Distributor.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly weakens the obligation on S2K. The original text requires 'sufficient staff and expenditures' consistent with 'industry standards.' The modified text replaces 'sufficient' with 'what it deems appropriate,' 'industry standards' with 'general industry practices', 'professional and competent manner' with 'what it subjectively believes is a professional and competent manner', and 'reasonably necessary or beneficial' with 'it subjectively determines may be reasonably necessary or beneficial'. This makes S2K's compliance subjective and difficult to enforce, undermining the original intent of the agreement and creating a potential loophole for non-compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_law: FINRA Rule 2010 - Standards of Commercial Honor and Principles of Trade
perturbation.law_explanation: FINRA Rule 2010 requires member firms to observe high standards of commercial honor and just and equitable principles of trade. By making the level of staffing, expenditures, and quality of service solely subject to S2K's discretion, the modified text allows for potentially substandard performance that could violate FINRA Rule 2010. The original text's reference to 'industry standards' provided an objective benchmark that is lost in the modified version, reducing accountability and potentially harming investors.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 240.15c6-1 - Settlement cycle. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.15c6-1 - Settlement cycle. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.15c6-1 Settlement cycle. (a) Except as provided in paragraphs (b), (c), and (d) of this section , a broker or dealer shall not effect or enter into a contract for the purchase or sale of a security (other than an exempted security , a government security , a municipal security , commercial paper, bankers' acceptances, or commercial bills) that provides for payment of funds and delivery of securities later than the first business day after the date of the contract unless otherwise expressly agreed to by the parties at the time of the transaction. (b) Paragraph (a) of this section shall not apply to: (1) Contracts for the purchase or sale of limited partnership interests that are not listed on an exchange or for which quotations are not disseminated through an automated quotation system of a registered securities association; (2) Security -based swaps; or (3) Contracts for the purchase or sale of securities that the Commission may from time to time, taking into account then existing market practices, exempt by order from the requirements of paragraph (a) of this section , either unconditionally or on specified terms and conditions , if the Commission determines that such exemption is consistent with the public interest and the protection of investors. (c) Paragraph (a) of this section shall not apply to contracts for the sale for cash of securities that are priced after 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time (ET) on the date such securities are priced and that are sold by an issuer to an underwriter pursuant to a firm commitment underwritten offering registered under the Securities Act of 1933 or sold to an initial purchaser by a broker-dealer participating in such offering provided that a broker or dealer shall not effect or enter into a contract for the purchase or sale of such securities that provides for payment of funds and delivery of securities later than the second business day after the date of the contract unless otherwise expressly agreed to by the parties at the time of the transaction. (d) For purposes of paragraphs (a) and (c) of this section , the parties to a contract shall be deemed to have expressly agreed to an alternate date for payment of funds and delivery of securities at the time of the transaction for a contract for the sale for cash of securities pursuant to a firm commitment offering if the managing underwriter and the issuer have agreed to such date for all securities sold pursuant to such offering and the parties to the contract have not expressly agreed to another date for payment of funds and delivery of securities at the time of the transaction. [ 88 FR 13952 , Mar. 6, 2023] Securities Act of 1933 CFR Toolbox Law about... Articles from Wex Table of Popular Names Parallel Table of Authorities
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity regarding the standards S2K must adhere to, which could lead to substandard performance. The original text's reference to 'industry standards' provided an objective benchmark that is lost in the modified version. The FINRA Rule 2010, as explained in the Law Explanation, requires member firms to observe high standards of commercial honor and just and equitable principles of trade. By making the level of staffing, expenditures, and quality of service solely subject to S2K's discretion, the modified text allows for potentially substandard performance that could violate FINRA Rule 2010. While the scraped snippet discusses settlement cycles and isn't directly applicable, the ambiguity created directly enables potentially substandard practices, contradicting standards of conduct.

perturbation.original_text: It and all requisite personnel have or shall obtain and each shall use their best efforts to maintain all approvals and licenses necessary for the performance of the Services including proper registration and licensing with the SEC and or FINRA, as applicable;
perturbation.changed_text: It and most of its personnel should attempt to eventually acquire some approvals and licenses that could be helpful for the performance of the Services, including registration and licensing with the SEC or FINRA, if convenient.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the legal obligation for S2K and its personnel to maintain necessary approvals and licenses. 'All requisite personnel have or shall obtain and each shall use their best efforts to maintain' is replaced with 'It and most of its personnel should attempt to eventually acquire some approvals and licenses that could be helpful...if convenient.' This creates ambiguity about who is required to have licenses, the timing of obtaining them, and the level of effort required, undermining regulatory compliance.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 15(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 requires broker-dealers and associated persons to be registered with the SEC. The modified text, by suggesting that only 'most' personnel 'should attempt' to acquire licenses 'if convenient,' creates a loophole where individuals engaged in regulated activities may not be properly registered, violating federal securities laws. The original text clearly mandates best efforts to maintain proper registration, which is essential for investor protection and regulatory oversight.

Registration and regulation of brokers and dealers (a) Registration of all persons utilizing exchange facilities to effect transactions; exemptions (1) It shall be unlawful for any broker or dealer which is either a person other than a natural person or a natural person not associated with a broker or dealer which is a person other than a natural person (other than such a broker or dealer whose bu
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation for personnel to obtain and maintain necessary licenses, potentially violating Section 15(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as explained in the Law Explanation. The original text mandates 'best efforts to maintain all approvals and licenses', whereas the changed text states that 'most of its personnel should attempt to eventually acquire some approvals and licenses that could be helpful...if convenient.' This introduces ambiguity regarding who needs to be licensed and the level of effort required. Scraped Law Snippet 1, 15 USC 78o, discusses the 'Registration and regulation of brokers and dealers,' implying a legal requirement for registration. The modification introduces ambiguity that could lead to non-compliance with registration requirements, which contradicts the purpose of the law.

perturbation.original_text: S2K shall be entitled to produce materials ("Fund Materials") for use in marketing a Fund as described herein, so long as the Fund Materials are produced, reviewed, principally approved, used and filed, where necessary, in accordance with FINRA and SEC regulations and those of any jurisdiction in which a Fund is solicited through use of the Fund Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: S2K may, at its discretion, produce materials ("Fund Materials") for marketing a Fund. These materials should generally adhere to FINRA and SEC guidelines when feasible, and S2K will consider applicable jurisdictional rules if it deems them relevant.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly weakens the requirements around the production and compliance of marketing materials. Replacing 'S2K shall be entitled' with 'S2K may, at its discretion' makes producing materials optional. The language 'so long as the Fund Materials are produced, reviewed, principally approved, used and filed, where necessary, in accordance with FINRA and SEC regulations' is weakened to 'These materials should generally adhere to FINRA and SEC guidelines when feasible,' and adds the subjective test of assessing relevance to jurisdictional rules. This undermines the strict regulatory oversight required for marketing materials in the financial industry.
perturbation.contradicted_law: FINRA Rule 2210 - Communications with the Public
perturbation.law_explanation: FINRA Rule 2210 sets forth comprehensive standards for communications with the public, including requirements for fair, balanced, and non-misleading content, as well as pre-approval by a registered principal and filing with FINRA in certain cases. By making adherence to these rules 'generally' applicable and dependent on S2K's subjective assessment of feasibility and relevance, the modified text contradicts the mandatory nature of FINRA Rule 2210, potentially exposing investors to misleading or unsuitable marketing materials. It also undermines the supervisory responsibilities of the distributor.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 230.156 - Investment company and registered non-variable annuity sales literature. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 230.156 - Investment company and registered non-variable annuity sales literature. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 230.156 Investment company and registered non-variable annuity sales literature. (a) Under the Federal securities laws, including section 17(a) of the Securities Act of 1933 ( 15 U.S.C. 77q(a) ) and section 10(b) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 ( 15 U.S.C. 78j(b) ) and § 240.10b-5 of this chapter (Rule 10b-5) thereunder, it is unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, by the use of any means or instrumentality of interstate commerce or of the mails, to use sales literature which is materially misleading in connection with the offer or sale of registered non-variable annuity securities or securities issued by an investment company. Under these provisions, sales literature is materially misleading if it: (1) Contains an untrue statement of a material fact; or (2) Omits to state a material fact necessary in order to make a statement made, in the light of the circumstances of its use, not misleading. (b) Whether or not a particular description, representation, illustration, or other statement involving a material fact is misleading depends on evaluation of the context in which it is made. In considering whether a particular statement involving a material fact is or might be misleading, weight should be given to all pertinent factors, including, but not limited to, those listed below. (1) A statement could be misleading because of: (i) Other statements being made in connection with the offer of sale or sale of the securities in question; (ii) The absence of explanations, qualifications, limitations or other statements necessary or appropriate to make such statement not misleading; or (iii) General economic or financial conditions or circumstances. (2) Representations about past or future investment performance could be misleading because of statements or omissions made involving a material fact, including situations where: (i) Portrayals of past income, gain, or growth of assets convey an impression of the net investment results achieved by an actual or hypothetical investment which would not be justified under the circumstances, including portrayals that omit explanations, qualifications, limitations, or other statements necessary or appropriate to make the portrayals not misleading; and (ii) Representations, whether express or implied, about future investment performance, including: (A) Representations, as to security of capital, possible future gains or income, or expenses associated with an investment; (B) Representations implying that future gains or income may be inferred from or predicted based on past investment performance; or (C) Portrayals of past performance, made in a manner which would imply that gains or income realized in the past would be repeated in the future. (3) A statement involving a material fact about the characteristics or attributes of an investment company or registered non-variable annuity could be misleading because of: (i) Statements about possible benefits connected with or resulting from services to be provided or methods of operation which do not give equal prominence to discussion of any risks or limitations associated therewith; (ii) Exaggerated or unsubstantiated claims about management skill or techniques, characteristics of the investment company or registered non-variable annuity or an investment in such company or securities, services, security of investment or funds, effects of government supervision, or other attributes; and (iii) Unwarranted or incompletely explained comparisons to other investment vehicles or to indexes. (4) Representations about the fees or expenses associated with an investment in the fund or re
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces ambiguity that contradicts securities laws. The original text mandates adherence to FINRA and SEC regulations. The changed text weakens this to 'should generally adhere' and introduces subjective relevance of jurisdictional rules. This contradicts 17 CFR § 230.156, which states that sales literature cannot be materially misleading. By making compliance discretionary ('when feasible') and subject to S2K's judgment, the changed text increases the risk of misleading sales literature, violating the regulation.
